headline: **MLB Daily Roundup: April 12, 2026**
article:
Shohei Ohtani's leadoff home run marked the 45th consecutive game with an on-base appearance for the Los Angeles Angels. Meanwhile, the Oakland Athletics unveiled a new home run celebration that has generated both excitement and concern. The Pittsburgh Pirates capitalized on a crucial error by the Chicago Cubs to secure a gritty series win.

Injuries continue to plague several teams, with notable absences including Baltimore Orioles catcher Adley Rutschman (left ankle inflammation), Tampa Bay Rays pitcher Joe Boyle (right elbow strain), and Minnesota Twins shortstop Royce Lewis (left knee sprain). These injuries will impact team lineups and rotations in the coming days.

Several teams made roster moves in the past 24 hours. The Milwaukee Brewers traded right fielder Steward Berroa to the Philadelphia Phillies for cash, while the St. Louis Cardinals acquired second baseman Brayden Smith from the Baltimore Orioles in exchange for left-hander Nick Raquet. Additionally, the Atlanta Braves acquired right-hander Victor Mederos from the Los Angeles Angels for future considerations.

top_news.title: Ohtani's leadoff HR a welcome sight in LA as on-base streak hits 45 games
A's new home run celebration is amazing, but also somewhat terrifying
First-place Bucs capitalize on Cubs' miscue in extras for 'gritty' series win
More history for J-Ram! 3B first Cleveland player with HR vs. other 29 MLB teams
Wacha's 8-inning scoreless gem drops ERA to MLB-best 0.43 mark
Tatis gets first career start at 2B, helps lead Padres to win
Lagrange hits 102+ mph NINE times in latest Triple-A start
Springer undergoing CT scan after fracturing toe on foul
